hypothesis: Given a semantic community's deterministic c-TF-IDF keywords plus 3 centroid-nearest titles, one small LLM call yields a short bilingual (en+zh) theme name that is more readable than raw keyword joins while staying presentation-only (keywords remain the auditable layer; labels never affect gating, grounding, or the ledger). Predicted: >=80% of community names judged specific-and-correct by the operator on the real ~17-community vault run, vs keyword-join fallback.

predicted_delta.label_readability: operator-judged specific-and-correct >= 0.8 on the real-vault communities
predicted_delta.cost: <= 1 cached call per community per themes.json rebuild

eval_plan.replay_set: themes.json communities from the real-vault crystal-themes run (keyword labels are the offline fallback, so every run has a baseline)

rollback: Drop --client live from crystal-themes (offline keyword-derived labels are the built-in fallback) and remove prompt.theme_label from evolution/components.json. themes.json is a rebuildable projection, so no durable state carries the labels.
